title: acer mono var. tricuspis
intro: Acer mono var. tricuspis, also known as Japanese maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Sapindaceae family. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, leaves, and roots, which contain flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Acer mono var. tricuspis have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tea, infusion, and tincture forms to take advantage of its medicinal benefits.
